name: Woods Hole Seafood Strudel
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds cooked shrimp, scallops, crab, or lobster, cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a Dutch oven and sauté onion and mushrooms until all liquid is gone. The final mixture should not be runny.
2. Add shellfish and fish and stir. Remove from heat and add parsley, ricotta, eggs, salt, and pepper; mix well.
3. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
4. Butter a large baking sheet or use a nonstick one. Place 2 sheets of phyllo pastry on the sheet and cover the rest with a damp cloth to prevent drying.
5. Brush the pastry lightly with melted butter, starting from the edges and repeating with remaining sheets, brushing every second one with butter.
6. Spoon the seafood mixture in a long even shape down the center of the pastry; carefully fold the pastry and roll into a strudel. Tuck ends under and brush with butter.
7. Reduce oven heat to 375°F and bake for 30 minutes, until the top is browned. Brush the top with butter twice during baking.
8. Cool slightly, slice, and serve.
